text.title: Trunk-Based Dev Principles Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns that surface when TBD adoption is declared without the substrate. Each has symptom, root-cause, and fix.

antipattern.symptom: BRANCHING.md says max 2 days; in practice 3+ branches are 1+ week old.
antipattern.root-cause: Policy is documented but not enforced by tooling. Long branches accumulate because nothing trims them.
antipattern.fix: Add a CI job that fails PRs from branches older than 2 days. Auto-close stale branches after a 7-day grace.

antipattern.symptom: p50 CI duration > 10 minutes for the past 30 days.
antipattern.root-cause: Tests + lint + typecheck were all serialized; or full e2e runs on every commit.
antipattern.fix: Parallelize: split unit / integration / e2e into separate workflows. Run e2e only on release-candidate or scheduled batches. Cache deps aggressively. Re-measure weekly.

antipattern.symptom: Incomplete work merges to trunk without a flag wrapper; users immediately see the broken state.
antipattern.root-cause: Flag plumbing absent; or author forgot to wrap; or no PR-time enforcement.
antipattern.fix: Add an "incomplete?" PR template field that requires a flag id when checked. Bot blocks merge if "incomplete" + no flag id provided.

antipattern.symptom: Red trunk persists > 30 minutes after author was notified; manual revert required.
antipattern.root-cause: Auto-revert was disabled "because it kept firing" — the underlying flakiness was never fixed.
antipattern.fix: Re-enable auto-revert. Quarantine flaky tests into a separate "flaky-suite" that does not block merge while their root-cause is being fixed. Auto-revert remains armed on the stable suite.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: An AI agent generating the readiness checklist must not inflate scores to please the user. Each evidence string MUST cite verifiable repo / CI artefacts (file path, run id, branch list) — not prose claims.
